messages.content:
Six million years ago, the Mediterranean Sea was
cut off from the Atlantic Ocean-this was the Messinian Salinity Crisis. For over half a million years, until the Zanclean flood refilled its basin, what remained of the Mediterranean Sea evaporated, forming a hypersaline landscape inhospitable to life, with surface temperatures estimated to be over 100 degrees Fahrenheit. Which choice completes the text with the most logical and precise word or phrase?
(A) temporarily
(B) briefly
(C) momentarily
(D) permanently
(A) temporarily

during
Artemisia, Queen of Halicarnassus, an Ancient Greek city-state, was praised by the historian Herodotus for the Persian Wars. Despite being Greek herself, she was a trusted military advisor to Xerxes I, the Persian King, and personally commanded a large naval force at the Battle of Salamis against an alliance of other Greek city-states. Which choice completes the text with the most logical and precise word or phrase?
(A) antipathy
(B) valor
2
(C) neutrality
(D) rashness
(B) valor

The Caliphate of Córdoba was an Islamic state in Iberia. When Hisham II became its third Caliph in 976 C.E., he was a mere ten years old, too young to rule. In his place rose Al-Mansur Ibn Abi Aamir, or Almanzor, an unofficial leader who conducted many military campaigns to push back expansionist northern Christian states while acquiring new territories for Córdoba; these efforts placed a heavy tax on the people of Córdoba, eventually leading to a series of rebellions that ended Córdoba's dominance in Iberia.
Which choice completes the text with the most logical and precise word or phrase?
(A) embezzle
(B) surplus
(C) burden
(D) policy
(C) burden

The Caliphate of Córdoba was an Islamic state in Iberia. When Hisham II became its third Caliph in 976 C.E., he was a mere ten years old, too young to rule. In his place rose Al-Mansur Ibn Abi Aamir, or Almanzor, an unofficial leader who conducted many military campaigns to push back expansionist northern Christian states while acquiring new territories for Córdoba; these efforts placed a heavy tax on the people of Córdoba, eventually leading to a series of rebellions that ended Córdoba's dominance in Iberia.
Which choice completes the text with the most logical and precise word or phrase?
a embezzle
b surplus
c burden
d policy
c burden

When she died of tuberculosis in 1884, Marie Bashkirtseff died as a relatively
twenty-five year old painter. Thanks to the posthumous publication of her diaries in 1887, which Bashkirtseff had diligently kept since she was thirteen, she gained the fame that had eluded her in life. Readers gained insight into her daily life, as well as feminist observations of European life of the late nineteenth century. Which choice completes the text with the most logical and precise word or phrase?
(A) tragic
(B) obscure
(C) controversial
(D) successful
(B) obscure

Native American hoop dancing has its origins in the healing ceremonies and traditions of many indigenous communities, but a style of performance hoop dance has emerged that incorporates modern innovations. Nakotah LaRance, a champion hoop dancer, designed movements inspired by influences as diverse as martial arts and hip-hop and included them in his dances. Celebrated at competitions and powwows, performance hoop dancing is rapidly growing as a way for indigenous communities to honor their traditions through creative artistic expression. Which choice best states the main purpose of the text?
(A) To explain how hoop dancers design their routines
(B) To discuss some aspects of performance hoop dance
(C) To call attention to the contributions of other dance styles to hoop dance
(D) To indicate how popular
hoop dance has become
(B) To discuss some aspects of performance hoop dance

The following text is adapted from Walt Whitman's 1868 poem "A Noiseless, Patient Spider." A noiseless, patient spider,
I marked, where, on a little promontory, it stood, isolated, Marked how, to explore the vacant, vast surrounding,
It launched forth filament, filament, filament, out of itself, Ever unreeling them, ever tirelessly speeding them. And you, O my Soul, where you stand,
Surrounded, surrounded, in measureless oceans of space,
Ceaselessly musing, venturing, throwing, seeking the spheres, to connect them, Till the bridge you will need, be formed, till the ductile anchor hold, Till the gossamer thread you fling, catch somewhere, O my Soul. Which choice best states the main purpose of the text?
(A) To celebrate the beauty of the web created from the spider's filaments
(B) To encourage the reader to persevere after numerous difficulties
(C) To warn the reader that life will contain many setbacks
(D) To suggest that the distractions of life may impede our goals
(B) To encourage the reader to persevere after numerous difficulties

The following text is adapted from Victor Hugo's 1911 novel Toilers of the Sea. Gilliatt is a solitary fisherman who lives in a remote island community.
Gilliatt brought home heavy takes of fish... If he had more than he wanted, he did not sell it, but gave it away. The poor people took his gift, but were little grateful... He was a fisherman; but he was something more. He had, by instinct, or for amusement, acquired a knowledge of three or four trades. He was a carpenter, worker in iron, wheelwright, and, to some extent, an engineer. No one could mend a broken wheel better than he could. He manufactured, in a fashion of his own, all the things which fishermen use.... his boat having but one anchor, he had succeeded, without help, in making another. The anchor was excellent.... In this way he had much improved the sea-going qualities of his boat. He employed it sometimes when he took a fancy to spend a month or two in some solitary islet. People said, "Ay! Gilliatt is away;" but this was a circumstance which nobody regretted. Which choice best states the main purpose of the text?
(A) To convey Gilliat's disregard
for the island's traditional way of (B) To describe the many ways (C) To explain how Gilliat became
life and the islanders' subsequent
rejection of him
Gilliat helps his neighbors and
contributes to village life
so different from the other islanders
(D) To contrast Gilliat's commendable qualities with the reservations of his neighbors
(D) To contrast Gilliat's commendable qualities with the reservations of his neighbors

Fans spend hundreds, if not thousands, of dollars to see their favorite singer in concert and anticipate the event with great excitement. But after enjoying the concert, many audience members are surprised to report that they do not remember significant parts of the performance, such as hearing their favorite song. Psychologist Ewan McNay explains that the brain interprets extreme excitement as stress and triggers the release of glucose, which the body uses to prepare for a fight-or-flight reaction, impairing the brain's ability to form and store memories. Although moderate excitement can improve memory formation, extreme excitement impairs it. Which choice best describes the function of the second sentence in the overall structure of the text?
(A) It summarizes McNay's most
significant point.
(B) It presents McNay's explanation that resolves a confusing situation.
(C) It introduces a question that McNay is unable to fully answer.
(D) It outlines a paradox that
McNay resolves.
(B) It presents McNay's explanation that resolves a confusing situation.

Bacteriophages, viruses that can attack and kill bacteria, may be a potent new weapon against strains of bacteria that have evolved to resist antibiotics. To determine if the use of bacteriophages is safe for humans, researchers surveyed the available medical studies that were performed since 2020. The researchers determined that, while antibiotic use can cause adverse effects in as high as 20% of patients, these rates are much lower in patients treated with bacteriophages. Which choice best states the function of the underlined sentence in the overall structure of the text?
(A) To summarize the results of (B) To present a specific example
the researchers' analysis
that illustrates the researchers'
findings
(C) To explain the goal and method of the researchers' study
(D) To identify a challenge the researchers faced in conducting the study
(C) To explain the goal and method of the researchers' study

Text 1
The life expectancy of the average male in developed countries is significantly shorter than that of the average female. Conventional wisdom explains that men age more quickly than women due to the presence of two x-chromosomes in women, which reduces the likelihood of a deleterious mutation. Text 2
Brent Graves and his research team argue that women age more rapidly than men and cite women's earlier loss of vision and reproductive ability as examples. They claim that other factors, such as a higher incidence of death due to the risky behavior of young males, contribute to the shorter expected life span of males in developed countries as compared to that of women. Based on the texts, how would Graves and his team (Text 2) most likely respond to the "conventional wisdom" presented in Text 1?
(A) By asserting that it fails to describe the mechanism used by the x-chromosomes to reduce the rate of mutation
(B) By arguing that there may be additional reasons for the difference in the life expectancies of men and women
(C) By suggesting that the duplication of x-chromosomes in women contributes to their more rapid aging
(D) By recommending further research to identify ways to decrease risky behavior among young males
(B) By arguing that there may be additional reasons for the difference in the life expectancies of men and women

Text 1
Evolutionary biologists have longed believed that a large number of mating partners contribute to the development of new species. The variation among mating pairs allows populations to differentiate, leading to more opportunities for the emergence of new species. Text 2
Josephine D'Urban Jackson and her research team compared the genetic diversity of shorebird species that mate with one partner for life to that of species that mate with a variety of partners. The researchers were surprised to learn that the species that mate for life displayed more genetic diversity. Since species that mate for life tend to return to the same breeding areas, Jackson and her team explain that the unexpected result may be due to those species gradually adapting to a particular location, and thus diversifying faster than species that disperse their genes over more locations through more mating partners.
Based on the texts, how would Jackson and colleagues (Text 2) most likely respond to the claims of the evolutionary biologists discussed in Text 1?
(A) By recommending that more biologists study the effect of migration distances on shorebird populations
(B) By asserting that it fails to recognize the specific characteristics that induce shorebirds to choose mates
(C) By noting that their own findings provide additional evidence that supports the traditional view
(D) By suggesting that their own findings indicate a need for further study of the factors that determine genetic diversity
(D) By suggesting that their own findings indicate a need for further study of the factors that determine genetic diversity

According to Indian economist and sociologist Radhakamal Mukerjee (1889-1968), the Eurocentric concepts that informed early twentieth- century social scientific methods-for example, the idea that all social relations are reducible to struggles between individuals-had little relevance for India. Making the social sciences more responsive to Indians' needs, Mukerjee argued, required constructing analytical categories informed by India's cultural and ecological circumstances. Mukerjee thus proposed the communalist "Indian village" as the ideal model on which to base Indian economic and social policy. Which choice best describes the overall structure of the text?
A. The text recounts Mukerjee's early training in the social scientific disciplines and then lists social policies whose implementation Mukerjee
oversaw.
B. The text mentions some of Mukerjee's economic theories and then traces their impact on other Indian social scientists of the twentieth century.
C. The text presents Mukerjee's critique of the social sciences and then provides an example of his attempts to address issues he identified in his critique.
D. The text explains an influential economic theory and then demonstrates how that theory was more important to Mukerjee's work than other social scientists have
acknowledged.
C. The text presents Mukerjee's critique of the social sciences and then provides an example of his attempts to address issues he identified in his critique.
